Gujarat has witnessed an intense start to the monsoon, with torrential rain lashing several parts of the state over the past 24 hours. While rain brought relief to Ahmedabad after a prolonged dry spell, heavy downpours triggered flooding, road damage and rescue operations in parts of Saurashtra and South Gujarat.
IMD issues red alert for seven districts
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a red alert for Gir Somnath, Amreli, Surat, Navsari, Valsad, Dang and Tapi districts, as well as the Union Territories of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu.
An orange alert has been issued for Dahod, Chhota Udepur, Narmada, Vadodara, Bharuch, Bhavnagar, Rajkot, Porbandar and Devbhumi Dwarka.
19 rescued from floodwaters in Junagadh
Heavy rainfall in Samadhiyala village of Mendarda taluka in Junagadh district left 19 people, including children and elderly persons, stranded in floodwaters while travelling in a rickshaw, a car and an Eeco van.
After receiving an alert through the 112 emergency helpline, the Junagadh Fire Station team rushed to the spot. Despite the strong water current, firefighters carried out a rescue operation under the supervision of the Divisional Fire Officer and safely evacuated all 19 people.
Rain returns to Ahmedabad
Ahmedabad also witnessed the onset of monsoon showers from Friday night. Several parts of the city, including SG Highway, Makarba, Juhapura and Sarkhej, received rainfall on Saturday morning under overcast skies.
Highway washed away in Junagadh
Meanwhile, heavy rain damaged a stretch of National Highway-51 near Husainabad on the Keshod–Mangrol route in Junagadh district, forcing authorities to suspend traffic. The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has started repair work, while motorists travelling between Somnath and Dwarka have been advised to use the Somnath–Jetpur–Porbandar–Dwarka route as an alternative.
Region wise rainfall record, South Gujarat records the highest rainfall in last 24 hrs
According to the latest rainfall data, South Gujarat received the highest average rainfall among all regions over the last 24 hours at 103.65 mm, followed by Saurashtra with 37.56 mm, East Central Gujarat with 26.99 mm, and North Gujarat with 26.89 mm, while Kutch recorded no rainfall. Since the onset of the monsoon, South Gujarat has received 263.88 mm of rainfall, or 17.11% of its seasonal average, followed by Saurashtra with 117.19 mm (15.38%), East Central Gujarat with 89.03 mm (10.87%), North Gujarat with 56.88 mm (7.79%), and Kutch with 2 mm (0.40%). Overall, Gujarat recorded an average of 43.71 mm rainfall in the last 24 hours, taking the state's cumulative seasonal rainfall to 120.62 mm, or 13.27% of its long-term average.
Top 5 Talukas to recieve highest rainfall
Junagadh district received the highest rainfall in the state at 565 mm (22.24 inches), followed by Maliya Hatina in the same district with 358 mm (14.09 inches). Vaghai in Dang recorded 341 mm (13.43 inches), Subir in Dang received 336 mm (13.23 inches) and Ambika in Surat district logged 335 mm (13.19 inches).
